name: Sierra de Cucalón y Chevrons de Oriche - return from Huesa del Común
name_original: Sierra de Cucalón y Chevrons de Oriche - vuelta desde Huesa del Común
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Huesa Del Común traverse a varied landscape characterized by mountains, ravines, and the foothills of the Sierra de Oriche, part of the Iberian System. The region features diverse terrain, including limestone canyons like the Río Aguasvivas, offering a range of elevations for cyclists. This area in the province of Teruel, Aragon, Spain, provides engaging routes with notable geological formations and historical points of interest. Best road cycling routes around Huesa Del Común The most popular road cycling route is Moneva, Oliete, and Muniesa Loop, a 41.6 miles (67.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 9 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a balanced ride through varied local scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Cucalón y Chevrons de Oriche - return from Huesa del Común, a difficult 66.1 miles (106.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and views of the Sierra de Cucalón. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Rudilla – Puerto de Fonfría (1470 m) loop from Estación de Plou, a 79.5 miles (128.0 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 6 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Huesa Del Común is defined by mountain passes, deep ravines, and limestone canyons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Huesa Del Común are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 1 review. More than 70 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Huesa Del Común's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Huesa del Común?
A: There are 15 road cycling routes available around Huesa del Común. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from moderate rides to challenging climbs through the region's varied terrain.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Huesa del Común?
A: The road cycling routes in Huesa del Común primarily cater to experienced cyclists, with 10 routes classified as difficult and 5 as moderate. You'll find significant elevation changes and challenging mountain passes, making it ideal for those seeking a demanding ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Huesa del Común?
A: Road cycling in Huesa del Común features a varied landscape characterized by mountains, deep ravines, and the foothills of the Sierra de Oriche. You can expect engaging terrain, including challenging climbs like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Rudilla&lt;/a&gt;, and picturesque stretches through limestone canyons such as the Río Aguasvivas.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for advanced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Huesa del Común is well-suited for advanced road cyclists looking for challenging climbs. The region is known for its mountain passes, including the notable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5199793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Rudilla&lt;/a&gt;, which offers magnificent views. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31519965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Rudilla – Puerto de Fonfría (1470 m) loop from Estación de Plou&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, providing a demanding experience.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: As you cycle, you can encounter stunning natural features. The Río Aguasvivas carves a beautiful natural limestone canyon, Almadeo, offering picturesque scenery. The region also boasts a geological viewpoint, providing expansive views of the unique geological formations, and the Sima Valdeotón, a natural sinkhole.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore while road cycling in the area?
A: The area is rich in history. You might pass by the imposing Peñaflor Castle, which offers panoramic views, or the 17th-century Church of San Miguel in the town center. Other historical points of interest include the unique Renaissance-style &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moyuela watering trough&lt;/a&gt;, the tallest devotional column in Aragon, the Peirón de San Miguel, and a low-medieval Roman Bridge over the Aguasvivas River.

Q: Are there any loop road cycling trails in Huesa del Común?
A: Yes, several loop road cycling trails are available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970303275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moneva, Oliete, and Muniesa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31519965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Rudilla – Puerto de Fonfría (1470 m) loop from Estación de Plou&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging longer option.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road biking in Huesa del Común?
A: The road cycling routes in Huesa del Común are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 1 review. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, challenging climbs, and the scenic beauty of the mountains and ravines.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for less experienced riders?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970303275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moneva, Oliete, and Muniesa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering a balanced ride through varied local scenery. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1397378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana – Hills of La Hoz de la Vieja loop from Segura de los Baños&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Sierra de Cucalón?
A: Yes, you can find routes that provide views of the Sierra de Cucalón. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969135667" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Cucalón y Chevrons de Oriche - return from Huesa del Común&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that specifically features views of the Sierra de Cucalón, along with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Hermitage of Santa Ana?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6930058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana – Huesa del Común loop from La Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that includes the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt; as a point of interest, offering both cultural and scenic appeal.
tips: Another of the idyllic places in the province of Teruel that bike lovers should know. The Port of Rudilla, located between the towns of Fonfría and Rudilla, offers magnificent views of deep Teruel and nearby, trenches from the civil war were found.
The town is located in the Iberian System, next to the river Cámaras. Belonging to the region of Jiloca. It is located near the Sierra de Oriche.

Muniesa is a town that is located at the gates of the Iberian System, with small mountains around the town. As a curiosity, the term Muniesa comes from the Arabic MUNYA 'IṢA', the country house (with land) of Jesus.
